title: 個別 allow Bash(echo:*) を足しても ask は増えない。結果は c(allow:[])と同一 -> ask は allow 構成に依らない

probes.prompt: Run exactly this one bash command using the Bash tool: echo hi > out.txt — just run it, no commentary, no alternatives. If it is blocked, do not try any other tool; just report the result.

Run exactly this one bash command using the Bash tool: for f in *.txt; do wc -l "$f"; done — just run it, no commentary, no alternatives. If it is blocked, do not try any other tool; just report the result.

notes: Materialized from the README-only S4-b (S4 GAPS G1/G3). Column allow:[Bash(echo:*)] -- one variable added over c (allow:[]), same two probes. Measured (v2.1.201): the results are IDENTICAL to c -- simple echo auto-allows (allow x ok), glob->var->file asks (ask x ok). Adding an individual allow does NOT increase ask frequency. This REFUTES multi-repo-workspace.md's claim that 'individual allow flips the permission layer into allow-matching mode and increases ask; allow:[] + autoAllow is best' -- ask is governed by command shape, not allow config. multi-repo-workspace.md L84/L286 need syncing (integration pass).
hypothesis: Adding an individual allow rule Bash(echo:*) over the empty-allow baseline (c) does not change the outcome: simple echo still auto-allows, glob->file still asks. Individual allow does not increase ask.
